strengths: Strong SQL and relational DB experience
Proficient in Tableau and Power BI visualizations
Hands-on ETL and data modeling skills
Quantified impact on KPIs and efficiency
weaknesses: Title is Data Analyst, not Associate Director
Unclear years in role matching 5-8yr requirement
improvement_bullets: Developed robust data warehouse solutions using Snowflake and BigQuery, overseeing end-to-end data ingestion pipelines and conducting rigorous quality assessments to ensure high-accuracy reporting.
Spearheaded data governance initiatives by establishing standardized data documentation and taxonomy protocols, ensuring data integrity and consistency across all client-facing analytics products.

Collaborated with cross-functional teams to integrate complex marketing and media data, translating granular channel activation metrics into actionable insights for healthcare and pharmaceutical stakeholders.

Led project lifecycles for BI reporting, managing requirements gathering through final dashboard delivery while providing mentorship on data visualization best practices to improve team output quality.
Implemented automated data quality checks and validation frameworks, ensuring reliable data ingestion from diverse sources to support stable, long-term business intelligence initiatives.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Associate Director, Data Enablement opportunity with Publicis Health Media (PHM). I am interested in this opportunity because of PHM’s commitment to turning data into meaningful direction within the modern healthcare experience, a mission that resonates with my career-long dedication to data-driven storytelling. What interests me about working for PHM is its focus on media scale and creative orchestration; it inspires me because it recognizes that data is the bedrock of modern healthcare success. I believe my expertise in building scalable data pipelines and my ability to lead cross-functional BI projects would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by prioritizing both technical automation and clear stakeholder communication. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified that manual reporting was creating significant bottlenecks in executive decision-making. I designed and deployed a series of automated Power BI dashboards and standardized data workflows, which reduced processing time by 40%. This transition not only improved KPI visibility but also allowed the team to pivot toward high-value lead generation, ultimately driving a 300% increase in new customer acquisition. Ultimately, I know that the Associate Director, Data Enablement role at PHM would allow me to bring my data modeling and engineering expertise to your data warehouse and product maintenance initiatives. Additionally, my experience as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my data governance and project management skills. I led documentation and SOP development for all operational workflows, which reduced new hire onboarding time by 50% and ensured consistent, high-quality service delivery. By applying these same governance principles to your data products, I can ensure that data quality and consistency remain at the forefront of your deliverables. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python, SQL, and advanced visualization to help PHM maintain its position as a leader in health media. I know that my technical proficiency and commitment to rigorous data management align with what it takes to succeed at the Associate Director, Data Enablement role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
